commit.message:
Add context tokens field to Local mode

Adds a persistent "Context tokens" field in the Local Settings box
(below the Model field, default 32768). At launch, the value is
injected as OLLAMA_NUM_CTX and LM_STUDIO_NUM_CTX so both Ollama and
LM Studio use the configured context window. Values below 32768 are
clamped. The setting is saved and restored across sessions.

fix: restore runtime imports stripped by Phase 8 type-import cleanup

RequiredFieldsForm and CalendarView are JSX components, not TypeScript types.
Marking them as `type` imports causes the bundler to strip them at compile
time, resulting in a ReferenceError at render. Removed the `type` keyword.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(github): use registry platform options during install (#8492)

## Summary
- Fixed registry-defined platform-specific options (like
`asset_pattern`) being silently ignored during tool installation
- Tools like flyway would select the wrong asset (e.g., alpine instead
of linux-x64) because:
1. `backend_options()` stored serialized TOML table strings as
`toml::Value::String` instead of parsing them back to
`toml::Value::Table`, so `get_nested_string()` couldn't navigate the
nested platform structure
2. `install_version_()` fell back to `tv.request.options()` (user config
only) instead of `self.ba.opts()` (which merges registry options)

## Test plan
- [x] `cargo test` passes (registry, github, tool_version_options tests)
- [x] `mise install flyway@12.0.3` now downloads
`flyway-commandline-12.0.3-linux-x64.tar.gz` instead of alpine variant
- [x] All linters pass

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Changes option parsing and install-time option precedence, which can
alter which release asset URL gets selected/downloaded for some tools
and platforms.
> 
> **Overview**
> Ensures tool installation uses the merged backend options (including
registry defaults) instead of falling back to request-only options when
`Config::get_tool_opts` has no override, so platform-specific settings
like `asset_pattern` are honored during asset resolution.
> 
> Updates `RegistryTool::backend_options()` to parse option values that
are serialized TOML tables back into `toml::Value::Table`, preserving
nested structures (e.g., `platforms.*`) rather than flattening them into
strings.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor
Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit
f2833dde04966b42970e4eab06b23e33068b6c03. This will update automatically
on new commits. Configure
[here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: pass original request URI to backend in nginx proxy

When proxy_pass uses a variable with a path suffix ($backend/api/),
nginx sends only the literal constructed URL without appending the
remainder of the original request URI. /api/seasons arrived at the
backend as /api/. Removing the path suffix fixes this — proxy_pass
$backend passes the full original URI unchanged.

commit.message:
Fix dark mode selector: html.dark :root → html.dark

html.dark :root uses a descendant combinator (looks for :root inside html.dark)
which matches nothing since :root IS html. Changed to html.dark {} which
correctly applies CSS custom properties when the dark class is on <html>.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Router._role_model_map not initialized in __init__ (#45)

The _role_model_map dict and litellm.suppress_debug_info were incorrectly
nested inside update_semantic_state() instead of __init__(), causing an
AttributeError when resolve_model() was called before update_semantic_state().

https://claude.ai/code/session_01JcidDv7naBujnmYssGnkTA

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: move torch.cuda.synchronize() INSIDE nvtx.range() blocks

Sync was outside the with-block so NVTX duration only captured kernel
launch overhead, not actual GPU execution time. Now sync is inside
each range so the NVTX timer includes the GPU wait.

docs: Remove all abbreviations from executive summary PDF

Expand every abbreviation for C-suite readability:
- MCP → Structured Data/Database
- GCC → Government Cloud (or Gov Cloud in tables)
- SP/PDF, SP/DOCX → SharePoint PDF, SharePoint Word
- KB/PDF, KB/DOCX → Uploaded PDF, Uploaded Word
- Com → Commercial, Pa → Partial, ER → Emergency Room
- DB → database, Doc → Document, P1-P10 → numbered legend

Drop Model column from scorecard (redundant with environment
in agent name). Add footnote explaining Gov Cloud = GPT-4o,
Commercial = GPT-4.1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix file watching: use PollWatcher for WSL2/DrvFs compatibility

- Switched from inotify (RecommendedWatcher) to PollWatcher with 2s
  interval — inotify doesn't work on /mnt/ filesystems in WSL2
- Track last-saved content to skip false reloads after our own saves
- Force-recreate editor on external change (clear cached editorState)
- Prompt user on external change when file has unsaved local edits

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix BlackHole gravity — visible grid bending, enemy pull, player pull

Grid physics retuned: anchor stiffness 50→15, damping 12→8, max displacement
60→120 so gravity wells actually displace grid points visibly.

BlackHole defaults increased to perceptible levels:
- Enemy pull 0.18→1.5, player pull 0.4→2.5
- Grid mass 80→250, per-absorb 18→40, radius mult 2.0→2.5

Grid anchor stiffness, damping, and max displacement now tunable via
settings panel sliders (read from gameSettings at runtime). BH slider
ranges widened to match new defaults.

commit.message:
feat: replicate antiapi memory system — lint tool, journal scaffold, MEMORY restructure

Patterns learned from antiapi memory system design:
- Separate cognitive biases from operational patterns
- Maintenance triggers documented inline
- [确认]/[推测] annotation culture for journal entries
- Automated health checks to prevent L1 bloat

Changes:
- scripts/journal-new.sh: create docs/log/ entries from template, enforces
  [确认]/[推测] annotation section, prompts index.md update
- scripts/memory-lint.sh: 7-dimension health check (L1/L2 line count,
  pattern/bias counts, link validation, journal aging, annotation stats)
- CLAUDE.md: add script usage to journal workflow section
- MEMORY.md (L1): restructure to 6-block antiapi pattern
    - Add 记忆管理策略 (MECE rules + maintenance triggers)
    - Split 陷阱库 → 项目特有偏差 + 模式库 (bias vs pattern, separate blocks)
    - Fix stale entry: init_state tiny-noise (not zeros) — updated after round 9 fix
    - Remove operational facts (MiniMax URL, H800 network) → already in L2
    - Improve 导航 with L3 doc entry points

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: revert nav buttons to original text-only sizing

Previous fix attempts added min-height:44px and justify-content:center
which were never in the original text-only design. The 44px was for
icon+text buttons. Text-only content is ~36px with padding:10px 4px,
so the artificial min-height created empty space making text float.

Reverted to the original commit 8830cd6 design: symmetric padding,
no min-height, no justify-content. FloatingTimer and content padding
updated for the shorter nav height.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement Slice 2: LLM integration, Feature Analysis workflow, CLI entrypoint

- LLM layer: ILlmClient, LlmRequest/LlmResponse, ClaudeLlmClient (HttpClient,
  no third-party SDK dependency, atomic Anthropic API calls)
- Feature Analysis contracts: FeatureRequest, RequirementsDocument,
  ImplementationPlan, PlannedTask, ApprovalDecision
- LLM handlers: AnalyzeRequirementsHandler, GeneratePlanHandler — both extend
  StageHandler<T,U>, include structured JSON system prompts, strip markdown fences
- Console approval handler: prints plan, prompts y/n, returns ApprovalDecision
- JsonFileRunStore: atomic temp-file-then-rename writes, indented JSON output
- FeatureAnalysisWorkflow: 3-stage definition wired via WorkflowDefinitionBuilder
- CLI entrypoint: reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, accepts description as arg or prompt,
  runs engine, handles WaitingForInput resume, prints summary, exits with status code
